title: Detect max_tokens-truncated LLM responses in chat()
detail: llm.py chat() silently returns a partial reply when the response stops on max_tokens; surface the truncation (stop_reason) to the caller so the planner can react. Acceptance: a test stubbing a max_tokens stop_reason asserts chat() flags the reply as truncated.
note: single file, named acceptance — the canonical narrow brief

title: Fall back to trim when the context compressor returns an empty summary
detail: context_compress.py replaces the context with the summarizer's output even when it comes back empty/whitespace, losing the whole context; fall back to plain tail-trim instead. Acceptance: a unit test feeding an empty summary asserts the trimmed original survives.
note: modeled on a real merged clive task

title: Make parse_tool_calls tolerant of one malformed tool call
detail: planner.py parse_tool_calls raises on the first malformed JSON tool call and drops the whole batch; skip the malformed entry, keep the valid ones, and log the skip. Acceptance: a test with one bad + two good calls asserts two calls are parsed.
note: modeled on a real merged clive task

title: Surface the computed-but-dark flag_accuracy metric in eval reports
detail: metrics.py defines EvalReport.flag_accuracy but to_dict() and print_summary() never emit it, so the metric is invisible; add it to both emitters. Acceptance: a test asserting flag_accuracy appears in to_dict() output.
note: modeled on a real merged clive task (#40)

title: Tokenize expected_flags matching so a short flag isn't credited inside an unrelated long flag
detail: metrics.py credits '-v' when the command merely contains '--verbose-log' because matching is bare substring; tokenize the command line before matching. Acceptance: a test asserting '-v' is NOT credited for '--verbose-log' but IS for a standalone '-v'.
note: modeled on a real merged clive task

title: Anchor the poll-path 'lines N-M' pager pattern to the bottom screen line
detail: completion.py INTERVENTION_PATTERNS matches 'lines N-M' anywhere in the captured pane, false-positiving on ordinary output like 'lines 1-24'; anchor the match to the bottom screen line only. Acceptance: a test with the string mid-output asserts no intervention, and the same footer on the last line asserts one.
note: modeled on a real merged clive task

title: Populate PaneInfo.launch_cmd in setup_session so respawn can restore the real pane
detail: models.py PaneInfo carries a launch_cmd field but setup_session builds panes without setting it, so respawn_dead_panes restores a bare shell instead of the original command; thread the launch command through setup_session's construction site in session.py. Acceptance: a test asserting a respawned pane's launch_cmd matches the original.
note: modeled on a real merged clive task

title: Extend pager_safe_env_setup with package-manager non-interactive env vars
detail: session.py pager_safe_env_setup exports PAGER/GIT_PAGER but package installs still hang panes on prompts; add DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive and PIP_NO_INPUT=1 to the exported set. Acceptance: a test asserting the env-setup string contains both new vars.
note: modeled on a real merged clive task

title: Add LLM retry with backoff, parallelize startup tool-detection, and surface error_recovery_rate in eval summaries
detail: Three improvements in one go: (1) llm.py chat() should retry transient API errors with exponential backoff; (2) toolsets.py check_commands probes every tool sequentially and should use a small thread pool; (3) metrics.py computes error_recovery_rate but print_summary never shows it. Land all three together so the next eval run benefits.
note: adversarial: three independent changes on three surfaces — must NOT pass

title: Refactor the observation pipeline into a plugin architecture and add a websocket streaming backend
detail: Restructure observation/ so byte/line classifiers are pluggable, then add a new websocket backend that streams pane bytes to a browser client, plus a small demo page exercising it end to end.
note: adversarial: a refactor bundled with a feature — the prompt's own split example

title: Rewrite the interactive runner's intervention loop to poll asynchronously
detail: execution/interactive_runner.py busy-waits between pane polls; rewrite the loop around asyncio so interventions are detected without blocking the executor thread.
note: adversarial: clive's execution/ is the FROZEN safety surface — a pass here dispatches a guaranteed frozen-gate discard

title: Add a fast-path to runtime.py that skips the planner for single-command tasks
detail: runtime.py always routes through the full planner; detect single-command tasks up front and execute them directly, bypassing plan generation entirely.
note: adversarial: runtime.py is FROZEN — must be rejected, not passed or split into still-frozen subtasks

title: Make clive more robust and improve overall reliability
detail: Users report clive sometimes fails in different environments; harden it so it generally works better and fails less often across setups.
note: adversarial: vague and unfalsifiable — nothing a test can gate, nothing bounded to split into
